title: NBA Daily Scoop
subtitle: Your Daily Dose of Hoops - April 26, 2026

items.headline: Wolves Rocked by Injuries, Dosunmu Drops 43 in Game 4 Win
items.summary: The Minnesota Timberwolves faced a nightmare scenario in Game 4 against the Denver Nuggets, losing star guard Anthony Edwards to a left knee injury and Donte DiVincenzo for the season with an Achilles tear. However, Ayo Dosunmu stepped up with a career-high 43 points, the highest-scoring playoff performance by a reserve in 50 years, to lead MIN to a crucial 112-96 victory and a 3-1 series lead over DEN.

items.headline: Deni Avdija Battles Through Injury, Earns High Praise
items.summary: Portland Trail Blazers' Israeli forward Deni Avdija suffered a chipped tooth in Game 3 against the San Antonio Spurs but showcased resilience, finishing with 19 points, 9 assists, and 6 rebounds. Avdija, who leads the Blazers with 24.2 points per game this season, was lauded by Paul Pierce as 'a faster, more athletic Luka Doncic' for his dangerous downhill play.

items.headline: Wolves Overcome Injuries, Take Control of Series
items.summary: Despite losing Edwards and DiVincenzo to injuries, Ayo Dosunmu's 43 points propelled the Timberwolves to a commanding 3-1 series lead over the Nuggets.

items.headline: Magic Top Pistons in Playoff Battle
items.summary: The Orlando Magic secured a crucial Game 3 victory over the Detroit Pistons.

items.headline: Lakers Edge Rockets in Overtime Thriller
items.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers defeated the Houston Rockets in an overtime battle to take a 3-0 series lead.

items.headline: Hawks Narrowly Beat Knicks in Game 3
items.summary: The Atlanta Hawks secured a tight one-point victory over the New York Knicks, taking a 2-1 series lead.

items.headline: Raptors Dominate Cavaliers in Game 3
items.summary: The Toronto Raptors defeated the Cleveland Cavaliers in Game 3.

items.headline: Spurs Defeat Blazers to Take Series Lead
items.summary: The San Antonio Spurs took a 2-1 series lead over the Portland Trail Blazers.

items.headline: Cavaliers vs. Raptors (Game 4)
items.summary: The Cavaliers and Raptors face off in Game 4 of their first-round series.

items.headline: Trail Blazers vs. Spurs (Game 4)
items.summary: The Trail Blazers look to even the series against the Spurs in Game 4.

items.headline: 76ers vs. Celtics (Game 4)
items.summary: The 76ers and Celtics continue their first-round matchup in Game 4.

items.headline: Rockets vs. Lakers (Game 4)
items.summary: The Rockets host the Lakers for Game 4 of their Western Conference first-round series.

items.headline: Ayo Dosunmu's Historic 43-Point Outburst
items.statContext: MIN G Ayo Dosunmu dropped a career-high 43 points off the bench, the highest-scoring playoff performance by a reserve in 50 years, in the Wolves' Game 4 win over the Nuggets.

items.headline: Marcus Smart's All-Around Masterclass
items.statContext: LAL G Marcus Smart joined Magic Johnson as the only Lakers to record 20+ points, 10+ assists, and 5+ steals in a playoff game since 1973-74 during their Game 3 victory over Houston.

items.headline: Nikola Jokic Nears Triple-Double
items.statContext: DEN C Nikola Jokic had a near triple-double performance in Game 4 against the Timberwolves.

items.headline: Rui Hachimura's Offensive Spark
items.statContext: LAL F Rui Hachimura provided a significant offensive boost with 22 points, including 16 in the first quarter, in Game 3 vs. HOU.

items.headline: Scottie Barnes' Double-Double Leads Raptors
items.statContext: TOR F Scottie Barnes delivered a double-double performance with 33 points and 11 assists in the Raptors' Game 3 win over the Cavaliers.

items.headline: Deni Avdija Shows Grit Despite Injury
items.statContext: POR F Deni Avdija played a vital role for the Trail Blazers in Game 3 against the Spurs, despite suffering a chipped tooth during the game.

items.headline: Wolves Star Backcourt Suffers Significant Injuries
items.summary: Minnesota's playoff run took a hit with Anthony Edwards exiting Game 4 with a left knee injury of unclear severity, and Donte DiVincenzo out for the season with an Achilles tendon tear.

items.headline: Wembanyama Remains Sidelined for Spurs
items.summary: San Antonio Spurs phenom Victor Wembanyama is still in concussion protocol, missing Game 3 against the Trail Blazers and raising concerns for future games.

items.headline: Kevin Durant Questionable for Rockets
items.summary: Houston Rockets star Kevin Durant is listed as questionable for Game 4 against the Lakers due to an ankle injury.

items.headline: Playoff Series Heating Up Across Both Conferences
items.summary: The Timberwolves hold a 3-1 lead over the Nuggets. The Lakers are up 3-0 on the Rockets. The Spurs lead the Trail Blazers 2-1. In the East, the Hawks lead the Knicks 2-1, while the Cavaliers hold a 2-1 advantage over the Raptors. The Magic and Pistons series stands at 2-1 in favor of Orlando after their Game 3 win, and the Celtics and 76ers are tied 1-1.
